message: A higher starting point in family resources is linked to a higher score destination.
In Ecuador, where a student starts matters a lot — the distance between low and high family resources is especially wide.
The path upward is clear in Guatemala: more family resources, higher average performance.
Students in Honduras do not begin from the same place, and their scores reflect that difference.
Paraguay highlights how unequal starting points can lead to unequal academic outcomes.
In Senegal, the pathway is less steep, but differences in family resources still shape performance.
Zambia shows that even when the gap is not the widest, starting conditions still influence where students end up.
